title: Acknowledgements, Delivery Tags, Redelivery, and Effect Ownership · Type-System Trace: Ack Race Single Owner
summary: A handler acknowledges on a different channel, auto-acks before validation, nacks every error with requeue, treats redelivered false as first-ever processing, and double-acks after a timeout race. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ack race single owner increment in delivery ownership and acknowledgement decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting.
Use redelivered and delivery-count evidence as hints while relying on stable application identity and durable deduplication for correctness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Ack Race Single Owner · Theory Ack Race Single Owner
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9360 in a quorum queue during a planned rolling maintenance window. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce same-generation, exactly-one terminal acknowledgement after durable effect; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use one terminal delivery decision owner to prevent timeout, abort, handler, and shutdown paths from double acknowledging or conflicting.
